title: How To Read Progress
concern: My child is practising, but I cannot tell whether improvement is real.
guidance: Look at accuracy, repeated weak topics and mock-test trend together. A single low score is not a disaster; repeated low accuracy in the same topic means the concept needs repair.
recommendedAction: Open the Results Report, check weak topics, then ask the student to revise one weak topic before taking another mock.

title: When To Book A Trial Class
concern: I am not sure whether my child needs regular classes or just resources.
guidance: A trial class is useful when the student has unclear concepts, repeated mistakes, low confidence or no study routine. If the student is already strong, a diagnostic plus targeted resources may be enough.
recommendedAction: Book a trial class with target test, class level and current weak area.

title: Choosing Notes And Practice
concern: There are many notes and resources. Which one should we buy first?
guidance: Start from the target test and weak subject. Buy premium notes only when the preview matches the student's need and the student will actually practise from them.
recommendedAction: Open Premium Notes, preview the relevant note and ask on WhatsApp before purchase.

title: Daily Study Routine At Home
concern: My child studies irregularly and becomes serious only near the test.
guidance: A small daily routine is better than occasional long study. The student should revise one concept, attempt a short practice set and record mistakes daily.
recommendedAction: Use Daily Plan, Flashcards and Error Log for a simple home routine.

title: Warning Signs Parents Should Notice
concern: How do I know preparation is going in the wrong direction?
guidance: Warning signs include repeating the same mistakes, avoiding mocks, memorising formulas without application, low reading accuracy and no revision record.
recommendedAction: Ask the student to take the Diagnostic Test and then review the Results Report.
